---
title: "About This Website"
---

I am a psychology researcher interested in the intersection of affective science and health psychology. This portfolio showcases my work in quantitative psychology and data analysis, with a focus on using computational and statistical methods to understand human behavior and health.

Projects include visualizations of health data, Bayesian analysis, creation of Shiny Apps, and multivariate techniques applied to health and affective science research. A central theme is using visualization not just to present results, but as an analytic tool. Plots are used throughout to check assumptions, identify patterns, and guide interpretation before drawing conclusions.

Methods featured include hierarchical Bayesian modeling in Stan, LOO model comparison, reinforcement learning parameter estimation, moderation and mediation analyses, and beyond.
